title: Today's Tech Brief: Rivian’s software chief thinks you don’t need CarPlay or buttons
summary: Rivian's software chief on voice control, Zig creator's perfectionism, and AI coding
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Rivian’s software chief thinks you don’t need CarPlay or buttons
imageCaption: Rivian’s software chief thinks you don’t need CarPlay or buttons (theverge.com)

body.text: According to an interview on The Verge's podcast, Bensaid believes that voice control is the future of car software. This makes sense, given the rise of voice assistants like Alexa and Google Assistant. But what's interesting is that Rivian is betting big on this tech. My read is that they're trying to create a seamless user experience, but it remains to be seen if consumers agree. And honestly, I'm a bit curious – would I use voice control in my car, or would I just end up yelling at Siri?

Next, the creator of the Zig programming language, Andrew Kelley, is holding out for 'uncompromising perfection' before releasing version 1.0. In an interview with The Register, Kelley expressed concerns about paying for cloud-powered AI coding, calling it an 'insane proposition.' I guess you could say he's a bit of a perfectionist. It's kind of refreshing to see someone prioritizing quality over speed and profit.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
